rationale.claude: Recognizably tailored — opens with the JD-mirroring title 'Bilingual Help Desk Technician' and surfaces the bilingual claim early. But the skills section keeps too many leftover-from-kitchen-sink items that don't earn their space for a help desk role (Operational Database Support including 'Neo4j - learning', 'Arduino', 'DMX Lighting', 'Interactive Laser Systems'). The Hospitality Service Technician role is included with very generic bullets that read as filler. Soft-skills section is 16+ items and includes entries like 'Resource Allocation' that aren't relevant to help-desk work. Reads as a draft that started from a tailored skeleton but didn't finish the cut.

key_strengths: JD-title mirroring in summary opener — recruiter scans see 'Bilingual Help Desk Technician' immediately
BEAT THE BOMB bullets cover the actual help-desk scope (ticketing, remote admin via RustDesk, end-user mobile support)
75% guest transition reduction metric is preserved in BTB section
AI Job-Application Pipeline project shows initiative and self-directed technical work
key_weaknesses: Skills section retains 'Arduino', 'DMX Lighting', 'Interactive Laser Systems', 'Multimedia Systems' that are noise for a help-desk role
'Operational Database Support (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Neo4j - learning)' — the '(learning)' hedge label leaked from internal truth-tracking into the final document
Hospitality Service Technician section adds page length without earning it — bullets are generic ('Performed self-directed diagnosis...', 'Addressed general property maintenance')
Soft skills runs 16 items including 'Resource Allocation' and 'Incident Response Planning' that don't map to a Tier 1 help-desk context
Projects section has 'The Organizer' (Downloads-folder file router) which is too small a project to earn a slot next to the larger pipeline project
specific_fixes: Drop Arduino, DMX, Interactive Laser, Multimedia from the skills line — they're BTB-specific and irrelevant to the JD
Remove the '(learning)' / '(Neo4j - learning)' parentheticals — internal truth-audit notation shouldn't reach the final doc
Either compress Hospitality role to 2-3 bullets with concrete scope, or drop it entirely (it's outside the 5-year relevant window for most help-desk roles)
Cut soft skills to 5-6 items most aligned with help-desk work (Customer Service, End-User Support, Technical Documentation, Communication, Problem-Solving)
Drop 'The Organizer' from projects; one strong project (the pipeline) is enough
tier_definition_match: Matches mid (~6.5): tailoring is visible but unfinished. The high-signal pieces (JD-title summary, BTB help-desk bullets) are there, but the editorial cut wasn't completed — leaving padded skills and a filler legacy role. Above 'bad' because tailoring is happening; below 'nearmiss' because content gaps and noise are substantive, not polish issues.
